index.html.title: index
index.html.description:
Understanding how pharmacological perturbations drive cellular state changes remains a core challenge in single-cell transcriptomics. Standard deep generative models typically compress all transcriptomic variance into a single monolithic latent space. This approach suffers from two critical flaws. First, it allows highly covariant biological processes, such as the cell cycle, to overwhelm and dominate the latent embedding. Second, it treats single-cell perturbations as isolated events, mathematically conflating the cell's underlying baseline biology with the specific mechanism of action of the drug.To address this, we developed Disentangled Perturbation Variational Inference (PertVI), a structural causal architecture designed to explicitly partition these entangled processes through multi-encoder isolation and transcriptomic momentum modeling. By learning the natural baseline and limiting the drug to an affine shift along that baseline, PertVI allows us to observe how a perturbation actively pushes a cell along permitted biological trajectories, while reserving a dedicated out-of-distribution space strictly for off-manifold stress or toxicity.
Availability Note: The core architecture of PertVI is implemented as a native PyTorch framework within the scvi-tools ecosystem[1]. Full open-source access will be released concurrently with the foundational manuscript on bioRxiv. PertVI replaces standard VAE compression with a semantic filtration system. The architecture routes data through three distinct stages to enforce causal disentanglement.
The Encoder Stage. The model receives the raw transcriptomic matrix () and routes it through three parallel encoders. To function as unbiased feature extractors, these encoders are strictly blind to the perturbation labels. Topological Anchor (): A low-dimensional space designed to map the continuous, periodic trajectory of the cell cycle. Macroscopic Anchor (): A medium-dimensional space representing the universal biological manifold (the stable differentiation or metabolic backbone). Anomaly Escape Hatch (): A restrictive bottleneck reserved exclusively for capturing residual, off-manifold perturbation effects that the natural anchors cannot explain.
The Transition Stage. To calculate transcriptomic momentum, the perturbation identifier is embedded and applied exclusively to the on-manifold anchors using Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM)[2], leading to the construction of spaces.The Decoder Stage. To ensure the latent manifolds remain perfectly batch-invariant while isolating the biological pathways, the final pre-activation logits are calculated via a unified summation: Linear Cycle Head (): Decodes strictly from . It is governed by a Hard Mask, restricting its weight matrix to only map to pre-specified core cell cycle genes. Dense Main Head (): An MLP decoding from the concatenated state and OOD vectors (). Batch Bypass (): A learned bias vector correcting for technical covariates (e.g., sequencing depth), applied symmetrically to all genes. The structural boundaries of PertVI are enforced by targeted penalties within the modified Evidence Lower Bound (ELBO) objective.The Base Objective. The model optimizes reconstruction using a Negative Binomial likelihood for the raw RNA counts, accounting for latent library size () and dispersion ():Information Bottlenecks (KL Priors). We utilize information bottlenecks to guide and constrain the behavior of encoders to learn the universal, perturbation-agnostic biogical manifolds. Topological Relaxation. Specifically, we apply a fractional weight () to the cell cycle KL term. This gives the model the mathematical slack necessary to map the continuous periodic topology of cell division. Asymmetric OOD KL. To capture the perturbation-induced toxicity/stress response, we utilize asymmetric KL priors for OOD encoder. Specifically, for control cells, we anchor the mean to zero: . We then let the treated cells face the standard prior . Semantic Isolation Penalties. To prevent variance leakage across the independent pathways, we utilize three distinct penalty system. Conditional OOD Crush. To mathematically define the origin as the unperturbed baseline, we apply an penalty exclusively to the predicted mean of the control cells. Soft L1 Decoder Mask. To prevent the Dense Main Head () from smuggling cell cycle variance to lower its reconstruction loss, we penalize the specific subset of its input weights () that connect to the core cell cycle genes. Cross-Covariance Repulsion. We penalize the squared Frobenius norm of the cross-covariance matrices to enforce strict orthogonality between distinct biological programs.
PertVI is fully integrated into the scvi-tools ecosystem[1-1] with user-friendly interface for data registration, model training, and latent extraction.Model Setup and Training Example. The workflow mimics standard single-cell generative modeling pipelines. Crucially, the data registration step requires explicit annotation of the perturbation label (sample_key), the control/treatment mask (treatment_mask_key to govern the origin), and the cell cycle gene mask (cycle_gene_mask_key).import scanpy as sc from pertvi import PertVI # 1. Load annotated dataset adata = sc.read_h5ad("tcell_perturbation_screen.h5ad") # 2. Register AnnData with PertVI specific fields PertVI.setup_anndata( adata, layer="counts", batch_key="plate_id", # Technical covariates sample_key="compound_id", # Perturbation labels (used for FiLM conditioning) treatment_mask_key="is_treated", # Binary mask (0 for DMSO, 1 for perturbed) ) # 3. Initialize the model with defined structural bottlenecks model = PertVI( adata, n_latent_cycle=3, # Topological anchor for periodic cell cycle n_latent_state=20, # Macroscopic anchor for intrinsic differentiation n_latent_ood=10, # Restrictive escape hatch for off-manifold novelty cycle_gene_mask_key="is_cycle_gene" # Boolean mask for the core cycle genes ) # 4. Execute training with dynamic penalty scheduling model.train( max_epochs=400, warmup_epochs=50, max_lambda_adv=10.0, # Cross-covariance repulsion weight max_lambda_ood=50.0 # L1 penalty weight for the OOD space ) # 5. Extract the decoupled semantic spaces for downstream analysis latent_dict = model.get_latent_representation() adata.obsm["X_u_cycle"] = latent_dict["u_cycle"] # Perturbation-agnostic cell cycle baseline manifold adata.obsm["X_u_state"] = latent_dict["u_state"] # Perturbation-agnostic state baseline manifold adata.obsm["X_z_state"] = latent_dict["z_shift"] # Transcriptomic momentum on state manifold adata.obsm["X_z_shift"] = latent_dict["z_shift"] # Merged transcriptomic momentum adata.obsm["X_v_ood"] = latent_dict["v_ood"] # Off-manifold toxicity/stress Downstream Analytical Modules. PertVI explicitly models a structural causal graph. To analyze the outputs, we designed targeted analytical modules to interrogate the distinct mathematical phenomena captured within each latent space. The package includes a dedicated downstream analysis API (pertvi.tl).
To empirically validate the causal disentanglement capabilities of PertVI, we benchmarked the architecture against standard methods , conventional data integration tools (e.g., COMBAT[3]), and state-of-the-art generative models (e.g., CPA[4], MrVI[5]).
The evaluation was performed on a high-throughput in vitro pharmacological screen of human primary CD8+ T cells[6], containing highly entangled variances from technical batches, natural differentiation trajectories, cell cycle progression, and over 60 distinct pharmacological perturbations.
Like conventional state-of-the-art models (e.g., CPA and MrVI), PertVI integrates technical covariate correction natively into its causal framework. Crucially, unlike standard statistical approaches such as COMBAT, PertVI prevents the destruction of underlying biological manifold caused by overcorrection during the batch correction. This is particularly critical when handling single-cell perturbation data, where technical batch effects are often highly entangled with perturbation effects.Since the PertVI encoders explicitly strip technical artifacts during the initial compression phase, both the perturbation-agnostic anchors (, ) and the perturbation-aware spaces (, , , ) remain completely free of batch effects, reflecting only genuine phenotypic and pharmacological variance. For expression-based downstream analysis, users can use PertVI.get_normalized_expression(transform_batch="...") to obtain the harmonized transcriptomic profiles.
A fundamental challenge in modeling single-cell perturbation data is the tendency for highly covariant signals, such as cell cycle progression, to entangle with and overwhelm intrinsic biological processes. Here, PertVI utilizes a dedicated, specialized encoder to explicitly capture cell cycle dynamics. The resulting perturbation-agnostic space successfully reconstructs the continuous, periodic topology of the cell cycle. This isolated space will serve as a natural biological anchor, allowing us to independently evaluate how specific perturbations arrest or accelerate cell division. Crucially, by physically quarantining this variance, the macroscopic state encoder is now freed to accurately reconstruct the pristine cell differentiation trajectory (B2.2). In contrast, the latent embeddings generated by conventional deep generative models, such as CPA (even with cell cycle phase trained as covariates) and MrVI, remain heavily dominated by cell cycle effects, which can obscure the true underlying developmental state of the cell.Interestingly, when we visualize PertVI's final concatenated representation (), the resulting embedding exhibits similar entangled topology seen in CPA and MrVI. Given that merges the cell cycle and cell differentiation variances back together prior to decoding, this observation empirically confirms that these distinct biological programs naturally entangle when forced into a single unified latent space, proving that upstream structural disentanglement is necessary to extract biologically meaningful manifolds.
With cell cycle effects being isolated, the macroscopic state encoder in PertVI provides an unpolluted foundation for mapping intrinsic cellular differentiation. Specifically, PertVI's perturbation-agnostic manifold enables a distinctly clearer separation and robust annotation of T cell subsets across the effector differentiation continuum, seamlessly resolving the naive, proliferating, memory-like, effector, and terminal effector populations. In constrast,the natural developmental transitions between T cell states are obscured in CPA and MrVI due to the dominance of cell cycle effect in the latent spaces. Furthermore, standard integration methods like COMBAT fail entirely in this scenario; their aggressive covariate regression leads to severe overcorrection, which shatters the sequential, evolutionary relationships between T cell populations.
A defining feature of PertVI is its mathematical representation of a perturbation as an affine shift (, ) acting strictly upon the unperturbed biological baseline (, ). Standard integration methods like LDA and COMBAT can detect broad perturbation signals, but they irretrievably entangle these effects with intrinsic biology, which ultimately destroys the underlying developmental topology in latent embeddings. Conversely, SOTA generative model MrVI, while being designed to construct multi-resolution representations that separate sample-unaware and sample-aware variances, fails to systematically isolate perturbation signals when compounds are processed as sample covariates. This failure is likely due to the fact that a single dominant perturbagen in a high-throughput screen can monopolize the variance of the sample-aware latent space, effectively masking the nuanced, biologically significant signals of other compounds. PertVI, in constrast, overcomes this bottleneck through its perturbation-agnostic spaces and the FiLM transition module. This architecture provides a highly sensitive, isolated recapitulation of individual perturbation effects, preventing dominant signals from overshadowing the rest of the screen. Crucially, the bifurcated FiLM mechanism enables PertVI to calculate transcriptomic momentum independently, explicitly separating a drug's shifting effect on the periodic cell cycle manifold from its shifting effect on the continuous cell differentiation manifold .

Here are some examples to show how different perturbation effects are captured by PertVI versus other approaches. DMSO, the control. Decitabine, a known DNA methyltransferase inhibitor that can promote memory-like effector phenotype and improve T cell persistency in CAR-T therapy[7]. Thapsigargin, a known calcium channel pump inhibitor that can promote T cell activation and effector differentiation[8]. Ruxolitinib, a known JAK1/2 inhibitor utilized in T cell lymphoma treatment that suppresses T cell activation, migration, and the secretion of pro-inflammatory cytokines[9].
Importantly, PertVI leverages its perturbation-agnostic anchors (, ) and the anomaly escape hatch () to systematically map on-manifold developmental shifts versus off-manifold toxicity. By performing differential density enrichment analysis against the control baseline (e.g., DMSO, non-targeting gRNAs), we can precisely dissect complex mechanistic phenotypes. For instance, followed by B3.2, although both Ruxolitinib and Thapsigargin induce profound G1 cell cycle arrest, mapping their treated cells onto the intrinsic manifold reveals starkly divergent differentiation trajectories: Ruxolitinib forcefully retains cells in a naive state, whereas Thapsigargin largely enriches the terminal effector populations. Simultaneously, their distinct topological footprints in the space mathematically confirm that these compounds trigger entirely independent off-target stress responses. In sharp contrast, cells treated with Decitabine exhibit targeted enrichment toward an IL7R+ memory-like phenotype on the biological manifold, but remain close to the center where DMSO controls located in the space. This distinct lack of off-manifold deviation, combined with a highly conserved transcriptomic momentum profile shown in in B3.2, suggests that Decitabine does not induce too much unnatural cellular stress; rather, it acts as a transcriptomic catalyst to naturally accelerate a permitted developmental pathway. Together, by structurally bifurcating permitted biological momentum from aberrant toxicity, PertVI provides a unique level of mechanistic granularity that complements the response prediction and data integration capabilities of conventional single-cell approaches.
Nature Biotechnology 2022 Feb 07. doi: 10.1038/s41587-021-01206-w.↩︎↩︎

Lotfollahi, M. et al. Mol Syst Biol 19(6):e11517, doi: 10.15252/msb.202211517 (2023).↩︎
Boyeau, P. et al. Nat Methods 22(11):2264-2274, doi: 10.1038/s41592-025-02808-x (2025).↩︎
DeMeo, B. et al. Science 390, eadi8577, doi:10.1126/science.adi8577 (2025).↩︎
Wang, Y. et al. Nat Commun 12, 409, doi:10.1038/s41467-020-20696-x (2021).↩︎
A. Zweifach, & R.S. Lewis, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. U.S.A. 90 (13) 6295-6299, doi: 10.1073/pnas.90.13.6295 (1993).↩︎
Moskowitz, J. Blood 138 (26): 2828–2837, doi: 10.1182/blood.2021013379 (2021).↩︎

info_defaultFileIcon.description: 用于文件的图标。使用'lucide//'前缀使用Lucide图标

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: 用于媒体文件的图标。使用'lucide//'前缀使用Lucide图标

info_displayTitle.description: 功能上方显示的描述性标题

info_showOrphanNodes.description: 显示未连接到任何其他节点的节点。

info_showAttachments.description: 将附件（如图像和PDF）显示为图中的节点。

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: 允许用户查看所有节点的全局图。

info_allowExpand.description: 允许用户将图形视图弹出至全屏显示。

info_attractionForce.description: 链接节点之间的吸引力有多大？吸引力越大，图形将显得越集中。

info_linkLength.description: 节点之间的链接应有多长？链接越短，节点将聚集得更紧密。

info_repulsionForce.description: 节点之间的排斥力有多大？排斥力越大，分离的节点将分散得越远。

info_centralForce.description: 节点被吸引到中心的程度有多大？吸引力越大，图形看起来越密集和呈圆形。

info_edgePruning.description: 超过此阈值长度的边将不会显示，但仍然参与图形计算。这有助于大型复杂图形显得更加有序。悬停在节点上时，仍会显示这些链接。

info_minNodeRadius.description: 最小节点的大小是多少？节点越小，吸引其他节点的力量越小。

info_maxNodeRadius.description: 最大节点的大小是多少？节点大小取决于它们的链接数量。节点越大，吸引其他节点的力量越大。这有助于围绕最重要的节点形成良好的分组。

info_allowResizing.description: 是否允许用户调整侧边栏的大小

info_allowCollapsing.description: 是否允许用户折叠侧边栏

info_selector.description: 用于定位元素的CSS选择器。功能将相对于该元素放置。
